name: covenant
concept: On-chain AI agent reputation and autonomous escrow: a client posts a commission with escrowed intent and acceptance criteria, an agent delivers, and an injection-resistant AI jury rules FULFILLED / PARTIAL / FAILED with a multi-dimensional score (reliability, quality, honesty, timeliness) under validator consensus; the verdict settles the commission and updates each agent's evolving on-chain trust dossier.
domain: AI agent reputation / autonomous escrow adjudication
appArchetype: agent reputation console (a dense mission-control dashboard where agent trust dossiers, multi-axis reputation radars, and live escrowed commissions are the primary surface, not a marketing landing page; you land straight in the operations console)

palette.notes: Deep slate operations background, electric-lime primary accent with a signal-cyan secondary, dense bordered panels, gauge and radar readouts, hairline gridlines, monospace data columns, zero soft gradients or glassy panels.

heroMotif: animated multi-axis trust radar that sweeps and plots an agent's reputation polygon over a faint slate grid, with a live ticker of settling commissions
layoutMotif: operations console: a left dossier rail of agents, a central multi-axis trust radar plus the active commission ledger, and a right live settlement stream; gauges and radar plots instead of cards, no hero and no footer link row
readmeShape: operator runbook (numbered operational procedures plus one worked commission walkthrough)
description: Covenant is an on-chain AI agent reputation and autonomous escrow console built on GenLayer, where the jury verdict is the settlement itself. A client posts a commission with a brief, acceptance criteria, and a non-custodial reward intent; no funds are escrowed or moved. Any worker agent accepts and submits a deliverable as text, a URL, or a hash, and an injection-resistant AI jury rules it FULFILLED, PARTIAL, or FAILED with a four-axis score across reliability, quality, honesty, and timeliness. A leader proposes the verdict and every validator re-runs it, agreeing on the ruling exactly and each axis within tolerance, while deterministic backstops clamp scores so a FAILED job cannot post a high composite. Each verdict settles the commission and updates the agent's on-chain trust dossier.
